construction.disjointness_note: 7 recon episodes are disjoint from the 10 hand-sample episodes used in eval set v2 (Phase 2/3 calibration). No episode overlap.
construction.sampling_method: Stratified random sample proportional to per-episode record count, adjusted so smallest episodes (C2E004, C2E036) contribute all records. random.seed(7777); random.sample per episode.
construction.verdict_methodology: Blind judge pass by Claude against locked spec §4 taxonomy and §5 Stage 0 rules. expected_* fields all null on input; populated by judgment on output.
construction.raw_text_policy: raw_text retained at full (NOT stripped). Stripping happens at calibration time (--holdout flag on test runner), not gate-set construction time.

gate.raw_text: "I would not recommend trying. Okay. Gustav, what's-- I can't read your last name. You've been given quite a debt for Trostenwald so you shall remain here until you've worked off the debt to the city. To the number of 2,645 gold pieces. To make up for the deaths of our two crownsguard, of the two civilians who have died, and the general cost of this entire investigation. We'll consider what your work is to the city tomorrow. However, the rest of you: you're free to go. I'm going to go get myself some sleep."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: This is even more awkward. Just put me back down. Put me back down.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Just let it happen.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: No! The chains are pulling me one way and you're pulling me the other way. Just come down to my level! Come down here!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "Bring the two others forward." They bring forward Gustav and Bosun. "This is your circus, right?" He goes, "It is my circus and I take full responsibility for what's happened. Sincerely. They have done nothing wrong. They are completely innocent. I brought this creature into our fold and that is on me. Whatever judgement you have, bring onto my head, not theirs. Please. I implore you." "Fine. I'll consider the investigation closed. You're all freed of charges against you. Including the goblin."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Oh! Thank you! I would kiss you if I could.

gate.judge_notes: Authority figure imposes a 2,645gp DEBT on PCs as judgment for crimes — negative obligation, not positive reward offer. Spec §3 explicitly cited this exact record as "durable anti-reward / persistence framing." Extractor has no negative-obligation detection; NPC voice + currency routes to QUEST_OFFER incorrectly.

gate.raw_text: "For four healing potions," and he pulls them out and plops them on the table, "240 gold pieces."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Look at this bruise.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "I cut fungus for a living."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Look, it's in the shape of a dick.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "I'm not going to ask. Four? Did you want them?"

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Sure, yes. How much for four healing potions?

gate.judge_notes: Sale price for 4 healing potions ("240 gold pieces" after "Did you want them?"). Players are PAYING, not receiving. Same family as hand-sample C1E099_t695.

gate.raw_text: Well, he's checked all his things, and he goes, "Okay, I thank you all so much for your help. I really appreciate it. You've saved my life."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'm sensing a lingering sibling rivalry.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: I know. It's carried over.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Hanging over the table.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'm clearly Lawful Good. Clearly.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: What's Thed doing?

gate.trigger_phrase: you find an additional 85 gold pieces
gate.raw_text: You stuff it all in there. Beyond that, going through them, for the sake of brevity here, you find an additional 85 gold pieces, 210 silver, and 45 copper.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yeah, I will quietly gather that up and put it into my bag.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay. Well, the armor's a full set, so it'll--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Am I able to carry that? I don't even know.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yeah, I mean, it's a leather chest plate, leather arm bands and bracers, and then leg plates as well, so it's a full set. You could probably fit all that into your bag if it's the only thing that's in your bag.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay.

gate.raw_text: 600 gold pieces, please. Eh, it falls under what would be general common clothes, so I'll put the cloak cost at two silver.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I would like to get something that is brown, muted, nothing gaudy. Probably don't have that anyway.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Not in this one, no. Your choices are pretty much brown, black, and gray, so you're good.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'll take the brown one. Yeah.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You're good on that one. I do not recall if they had any pricing put down for cloaks.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's 50 gold.

gate.judge_notes: "600 gold pieces, please" is Matt giving the price for an item player is buying (cloak). Not loot. Currency + Matt voice routed to MATERIAL_LOOT incorrectly.

gate.raw_text: As the fangs dig into the side of your torso, you pull back and try and defend yourself and it pierces your leather armor in your torso. It releases a huge burst of venom into your chest. You feel your lungs and heart seize for a moment and you almost fall back. That burning orcish rage from underneath gives you that last bit of inspiration to shrug off the poison and look back angrily and realize you are in deep shit.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: All right, you take 15 points of poison damage.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: That's all of the poison damage.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'm unconscious. No! Relentl-- The thing, I got the thing! It's called Relentless Endurance! I come back to one hit point!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's Relentless!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Sorry, I'm half dead.

gate.judge_notes: Spec §3 cites this exact record as MECHANICAL_GRANT, but reading is narrative flavor on a player-triggered Relentless Endurance feat. Reasonable mark either way.

gate.raw_text: He goes, "Well, thank you for your honesty. Anything else?" He gives a very intense look across each of your faces. "You would like to inform me that you found, encountered, any warnings? We're going back in after there to clear out whatever remains behind so we want to make sure we're prepared."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Come on, Jester.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns_last5.text: I didn't keep mine.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yeah, I know.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Look, we know who has them. We know.

gate.raw_text: "We shall see. Anyway, you musn't be seen dilly dallying along at this home, just in case. Thank you for the update, thank you for Horris, and good luck, friends. I do hope we meet again soon, in better times."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Of course. It was the least we can do. We trust with this information and the right things occurring in the next few days, you can find your way to some prosperous moves for this city.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "I hope so."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: A lot of bad things are happening now. It would be nice if one good thing could come of it all.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "That would be. I'd like to see a turn for the right in this city, or another, at least. There is right in this city. There are good people. As terrible as the death of the High-Richter may be, in essence I think much good may come out of it, in the long run."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's entirely up to you now, isn't it?

gate.raw_text: "We shall see. Anyway, you musn't be seen dilly dallying along at this home, just in case. Thank you for the update, thank you for Horris, and good luck, friends. I do hope we meet again soon, in better times."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Of course. It was the least we can do. We trust with this information and the right things occurring in the next few days, you can find your way to some prosperous moves for this city.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "I hope so."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: A lot of bad things are happening now. It would be nice if one good thing could come of it all.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "That would be. I'd like to see a turn for the right in this city, or another, at least. There is right in this city. There are good people. As terrible as the death of the High-Richter may be, in essence I think much good may come out of it, in the long run."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's entirely up to you now, isn't it?

gate.raw_text: Stunned means you can't really do anything this turn, but half make a sentence. You're aware of your surroundings but you can't take actions or move. At the end of your turn, it wears off.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Wait, I have a question. When we're paralyzed, does that mean every attack against us is a critical?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Every melee is, if it hits. That ends its turn. That brings us to Beau. You're paralyzed, so at the end of your turn it fades. Sorry, not paralyzed, stunned. It's not paralyzed. It's stunned.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Stunned.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Correct. It is not-- I had to read--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: What can I do?

gate.judge_notes: Matt explaining the Stunned status condition rules ("You're aware of your surroundings but you can't take actions or move"). Rules-adjudication DISCOURSE. Phase 3 rules-talk patches do not catch status-condition explanation shape.

gate.raw_text: No, there's nothing else. You look down into the box. There are sets of gold and silver rings and jewelry. It looks like whatever jewelry once may have been dressed upon the individual who was interred in that urn that was scattered. This is where their jewelry was placed.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: There we go. (laughter)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's a five feet by five?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's five by five.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: You're in a tiny-- Why don't you bring that thing out of there?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: No! I've got this! I'm fine! I'll find the treasure! There's nothing else in there?

gate.judge_notes: Spec §3 cites this as ENVIRONMENTAL_DISCOVERY example, but spec §4 admits borderline with MATERIAL_LOOT (party can take items from open box). Either reading defensible.

gate.raw_text: "And the like, I'm sure of it. My contacts in town are refusing to investigate, recounting howls from the deep swamp. I would need you to head up to Berleben. Locate a fellow named Febron Keyes, usually hangs around the Keystone Pub in the Puddles. The Puddles is the part of the town that is, heh, sinking. He's my point man for shipments, and the only one who knows where our safe house in the swamp is that hasn't gone fucking missing. Meet with him, secure the safe house and its contents, and return to me. Consider a 250 gold piece advance and a purse of 1,750 gold pieces upon completion. Heck, if you can handle both (laughs) and with timely mind, there might be a bonus."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "Understood. That underground tunnel, that you've already visited, happens to be a major artery for our smuggling business, leading straight up to the Lebenda Swamp. The Lebenda Swamp is a useful place to lose things or people, until you need to find them. As well as a direct route to my contacts in Berleben. We've been rather worried that no shipments have made their way south by the river in weeks. After sending your friend up to Berleben last week, it was discovered that our safe house and storage facility in the swamp has gone dark. One of my men, as well as a handful of theirs in Berleben, went to investigate and never returned. Now the swamp-folk are a superstitious lot, an unfortunate facet with working with them."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Like swap creatures?

gate.raw_text: "A sovereign town, outside of the Dwendalian Empire and outside of the law. That uses the Savalierwood to keep the Crown at bay. Spoken of as a haven for criminals, thieves, murderers, but really, it's home to the truly free entrepreneurs of Wildemount... and criminals, thieves, and murderers. Now, Ophelia is unable to enact on any partnerships at the moment, as her current business is tangled up in some of the local riffraff and she's requested aid in stabilizing her situation. What I propose, as you've already proven yourselves effective problem solvers, self-starters with enough sense of honor to see a deal through, is for you all to travel northward, past the Silberquel Ridge to Shadycreek Run to parlay with Ophelia. Help her in absolving her from her current mire of complications and return with her here to Zadash. For this task completed, I offer an advance of 500 gold pieces and a purse of 4,500 gold pieces upon returning with Miss Mardun within the month."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Fucking Louis.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "Indeed." You glance back over your shoulder and see Louis at the bar, who's still dazed and "drinking a giant cup of ale and wiping the blood off of his whole torso. "If you've interest in "making coin, doing some interesting work and getting to see most of our beautiful, imperial countryside, I can go on."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Ja, who are these friends of yours up north?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "An old friend of mine, named Ophelia Mardun. She has a base of operations about 350 miles northeast of here. Past Nogvurot and the Quannah Breach, on the outskirts of Shadycreek Run. I'm sure you've heard of it."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yes, we have, actually.

gate.raw_text: "1200 gold pieces."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Bracers?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "Oh, yeah. Right over here." Goes and pulls one of the boxes from the shelves. It has a glass top and he presents it to you. There's a velvet interior with two little alcoves inside where these very beautiful, silver bracers with scrolling and runic sigils carved into it across the band are present, "These would make a better defensive capability for someone who does not themselves partake in the use of armor."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Oh. I don't partake in the use of armor.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "Well, that sounds like it's meant to be."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Uh-huh. How much?

gate.judge_notes: "1200 gold pieces" said by NPC after Marisha asks "How much?" about armor. Sale price, not quest offer. Same family as RECs 2, 5.

Spoken of as a haven for criminals, thieves, murderers, but really, it's home to the truly free entrepreneurs of Wildemount... and criminals, thieves, and murderers. Help her in absolving her from her current mire of complications and return with her here to Zadash. preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "An old friend of mine, named Ophelia Mardun. She has a base of operations about 350 miles northeast of here. Past Nogvurot and the Quannah Breach, on the outskirts of Shadycreek Run. I'm sure you've heard of it."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yes, we have, actually.

gate.raw_text: As she pushes you all, she's not answering any more questions. She's like, "Out, out, out, out. You, both of you." Her hands pass through your duplicitous image. She goes, "That's strange. Okay, out. Thank you for the tea, goodbye, goodbye, goodbye."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "Good. Go!"

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Come on, Jessie!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay. Shit.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: I've mastered standing so still.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: But you know, a full-on library, or perhaps, you know, a smutty bookshop even, because you never know what you're going to find in there. Or even a used, a vintage book-- Oh, you're not even-- (shouting) Are there any bookstores in the vicinity of this lighthouse?

gate.judge_notes: "Thank you for the tea, goodbye, goodbye" is dismissive politeness phrase, not substantive gratitude expression. Trigger fires correctly on real "thank you for X" in NPC voice; substantive-reward reading is debatable.

gate.raw_text: "You're not wrong. But though he may be gone, he left me with the greatest gift I've ever had, so--" She runs her hands through your hair once more. "For that, I'm thankful."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: I didn't even think to ask about him in Zadash, because I thought he would be in the ocean.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: In the ocean?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yeah, he told Mama he was in the ocean!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "Said he had to leave and take care of some business on the sea. I never saw him again."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Just like a man.

gate.raw_text: There you go. They once marked the exterior of the original city-- I will fucking kill you. Never mind, you don't get lore! I can give and I can take away! No, the Opal Archways mark what were the original boundaries of the city when it was first founded, and the city has expanded far beyond that, but that now marks the richer, most lively district where a lot of the political elite, where a lot of diplomats, and the wealthy merchants come to either live and/or spend their money. You pass beyond this archway, the original, occasional set poles that hold the oil lanterns that give the faint bit of flickering firelight through these dark cobblestone streets give way to much more vibrant, magical baubles that hover about ten feet above the air. They've already been set, and you see them give off a brighter glow, and the colors seem to shift. One of them has a whiter color but then slowly turns to an orange, then shifts to a green. About ten feet away on the other side of the street, you see another one that's colors are shifting but in a different pattern, so they're kind of complementing and also contrasting each other. At this point, too, you begin to see the streets-- while not as crowded as the wharf, people that are here are much better dressed. You see a carriage make its way by to your right, and you see a few folks that appear to be well-dressed nobles of some kind, wearing a crushed silk-type clothing that is bright blues and purples, and jewelry of all sorts of gems and colors. Compared to a lot of the drab decor that a lot of the noble families wear in your experiences up in the Empire, the cultural mishmash that tends to build here on the coast over time with so many people coming from so many places, it all has built this mixing pot of styles and ideas and self-expression, and it immediately is visible in the people that live here. You do notice, strangely, that there are a few townsguard among the zolezzo here.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Opal Archway?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: There are eleven of these throughout the district.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: One of the Opal Archways? Wow.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: These, if you were to recount what information of history you know about the city here, the Opal Archways once marked the-- goddamn it.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: No, you go. I'll just repeat it.

gate.raw_text: I'll allow it.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay. There's Caduceus. Finishing your go. Yasha's up.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yasha's going to rush up and aid Beau in trying to pull Marius towards the docks.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay. If she wants to, she can attempt to grapple and then pull him out of Beau's grip, or we can say she holds her action and then-- it gets a little weird, because it's-- movement is what you pull him out of the way.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Would it help make full speed when it comes back to Beau's turn if she's assisting? Can they double pull?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Can she go, and then I assist on my turn?

gate.trigger_phrase: you find a pouch of 20 platinum pieces
gate.raw_text: Okay, you find a pouch of 20 platinum pieces.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: No.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'll pat him down.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: By this point, by the way, everyone has 16 hit points added to their--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: 18 for investigation.

gate.raw_text: 12, okay. Rifling through his bits, you find 21 gold pieces, 45 silver pieces, two platinum pieces.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Let's just sit here until it's Crash Pandas tomorrow.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'll lean over to Nott and say  Nott, do me a favor and make sure he's not carrying anything fancy in his pockets.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'm going to rifle through all of his pockets with lightning speed agility of hand.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Make an investigation check, please.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Mm, not good. 12.

gate.raw_text: You find another pouch that's hidden within his belt pouch that contains 62 gold pieces, three silver, 50 copper, and what looks to be a pouch of chewing tobacco.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: 18 for investigation.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay, you find a pouch of 20 platinum pieces.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: That's the money he was going to return.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: That's a lot.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: That's what he got paid.

gate.raw_text: She doesn't have the chance to respond to the first one, because you go right through to the second, but Marion does respond to the second and goes, "Oh! Well, thank you for letting me know, Jester. Be safe, be bright, and make me proud. And come back soon. You and your friends. Goodbye!" All right, who's keeping watch the first evening? Because now, with a full crew, you can go full pace on this longship, which will put you about 72 miles per day. It's about a 250 nautical mile trip, including through the reef, with a full crew. That means it'll put you there in a little over three days, about three and a half, maybe four days with careful passage through the reef. For the evening, who's watch? Okay. And you have crew who can take up as well, if you want to. So one of you or both of you, make a perception check.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay. All right. The evening comes quick; by the time you guys are leaving, it's not that far from dusk, and so your first evening out on your new trajectory, you have time. What do you want to send to Marion?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Mama, stuff happened at the docks and we had to leave very quickly. Please take care of Nugget for a while. We'll be back, I promise. You may have heard about the little girl who was killing herself on the dock--

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Are you using this across multiple spells?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yes, this is my second message. My second message: that looked like me but it wasn't me, don't worry, I'm very much alive, and I love you very much, and I'll be home soon.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Beep!

gate.raw_text: That would be you, I think, Fjord. Make an intelligence check and I'll give you advantage, because you have a sailor background and you understand some of the pathways, especially since watching and noticing this earlier with Caduceus, you've seen it move back and forth.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: We drop sail, we take off and I leave you guys in the cove.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: With one person?!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yep.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay, as you're making your way towards Nicodranas having this conversation, the Zhelezo ship is doing its patrol across. You have to time the best way to come through without gaining its attention. Who wants to be in charge of trying to navigate the ship?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: You, you're the sailor.

gate.judge_notes: "Make an intelligence check and I'll give you advantage" — the trigger fired on generic "I'll give you" and routed to MATERIAL_LOOT instead of MECHANICAL_GRANT. Object-aware routing on "I'll give you" needed.

notes: Gate-set v2 with blind-judge verdicts by Claude. Strict precision = correct/total. NONE category = should-have-been-rejected (DISCOURSE reject family) OR misclassification target (e.g. MECHANICAL_GRANT routed to MATERIAL_LOOT).
